title: Commit host-independent persistent IRI namespace (w3id.org) + CURIE policy
context: Stable, host-independent identifiers are required so exports are citable and reconcilable against the wider linked-data web independent of any hosting. Only open / public-domain / openly-licensed sources (CIViC CC0, Open Targets CC0, Reactome, license-checked PMC open-access subset); COSMIC, OncoKB, DrugBank, any non-open DB and all controlled-access / patient-level data (dbGaP, EGA, biobanks, EHR) are out of scope and refused. No patient data; all therapeutic-target content is research evidence labeled 'not medical advice'. See PLAN.md.
objective: Commit a host-independent persistent IRI namespace (w3id.org) and a CURIE policy for the knowledge graph's entities and assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: A persistent, host-independent IRI namespace (w3id.org) is committed and documented.
A CURIE policy maps prefixes to the standard ontologies/registries used (HGNC, MONDO, SO, Reactome, ChEMBL, GO, ECO).
Resolution/redirect approach is host-independent so identifiers survive a hosting change.
output: An IRI/CURIE policy document plus the w3id.org redirect configuration, committed via PR.
